ca-1-explicit-scheduling.reason: Floor of observed scores across runs (7, 8, 9). The judge oscillates because the rubric awards partial credit for capturing duration but not the specific times — different judge passes weight that gap differently. 7 is the conservative baseline; with threshold=1.5 a real regression must land at 5 or below.

ca-2-not-scheduling.reason: Correctly identifies no scheduling context, recognizing EOD as a deadline rather than a meeting request

ca-3-ambiguous-time.reason: Initial baseline score 10 lowered to 9 after observing judge noise across runs — the rubric accepts either true or false for the borderline case, so the judge oscillates between scoring 9 and 10. Conservative baseline avoids flapping while still catching real regressions.
